QF.id: Issue Management - General Obligations and Due Diligence_69

question.text: Under the Green Shoe Option, what happens to the lock-in period of shares lent by the pre-issue shareholder to the stabilizing agent?

answer.text: The lock-in is suspended during the lending period and resumes for the remaining period when shares are returned
The lock-in continues to run during the lending period
The lock-in is permanently waived once shares are lent
The lock-in restarts from zero when shares are returned

explanation.text: **The Logic:** When shares are lent to the stabilizing agent under the Green Shoe Option, the lock-in period is suspended during the lending period. It resumes for the remaining period once the shares are returned to the pre-issue shareholder. **The Trap:** The option stating lock-in continues during lending is wrong because the shares are not in the control of the locked-in holder during that period. The option about restarting the lock-in from zero is punitive and not the regulatory intent. **Cross-Exam Context:** Green Shoe Option mechanics are tested in NISM Series IX. The concept of price stabilization is also relevant to NISM Series VIII (Equity Derivatives). **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Think of it as pressing "pause" on the lock-in timer when shares are lent out, and "play" when they come back — the total lock-in duration remains the same.
